chunks.text: On the morning when we reached the airport, my friend was waiting
going abroad for a new position.
Loading his luggage on the cart, he turned toward me.
Though smiling, his eyes seemed a little wet.
We gave each other a brief hug and exchanged a small promise.
Walking toward the gate, he did not turn back.
Bowing in my heart, I saw him off.
Not turning back was probably the better way, I thought.
My friend, having spent many years with me, was leaving for his new pursuit.
We would keep in contact, we had promised.
Through the large glass window of the airport, I watched his plane depart.
In the morning light, the plane slowly rose into the sky.
The future that was carrying my friend seemed to spread beyond the clouds.
